report_title: OpenAI's Latest Product Developments and Strategic Partnerships
executive_summary: The introduction of GPT-5.2-Codex and the partnerships with AWS and Broadcom demonstrate OpenAI's commitment to advancing AI technology and providing innovative solutions for various industries [1] [2] [8]. OpenAI's focus on safety and security is also evident in its approach to deploying GPT-5.2-Codex, which includes gradual rollout and safeguards to maximize defensive impact while reducing the risk of misuse [1].

key_findings.finding_summary: OpenAI has introduced GPT-5.2-Codex, a advanced agentic coding model for professional software engineering and defensive cybersecurity [1]. This model is optimized for agentic coding in Codex and includes improvements on long-horizon work through context compaction, stronger performance on large code changes, and improved performance in Windows environments [1]. GPT-5.2-Codex is expected to enhance the capabilities of OpenAI's Codex platform [1].

OpenAI has announced a multi-year strategic partnership with AWS to provide world-class infrastructure to run and scale OpenAI's core artificial intelligence (AI) workloads [2]. The partnership will enable OpenAI to access AWS compute comprising hundreds of thousands of state-of-the-art NVIDIA GPUs [2]. This collaboration is expected to enhance OpenAI's AI capabilities and provide cost and performance optimized next-generation AI infrastructure [2].

OpenAI has partnered with Broadcom to deploy 10 gigawatts of OpenAI-designed AI accelerators [8]. The collaboration will power breakthroughs in AI and bring the technology's full potential closer to reality [8]. The partnership will also enable OpenAI to embed what it has learned from developing frontier models and products directly into the hardware, unlocking new levels of capability and intelligence [8].

OpenAI has invested in Merge Labs, which will accelerate research and development in bioengineering, neuroscience, and device engineering [7]. The investment will also enable Merge Labs to benefit from AI operating systems that can interpret intent, adapt to individuals, and operate reliably with limited and noisy signals [7].

OpenAI's approach to deploying GPT-5.2-Codex includes gradual rollout and safeguards to maximize defensive impact while reducing the risk of misuse [1]. The company's focus on safety and security is also evident in its partnerships with AWS and Broadcom, which will enable OpenAI to provide cost and performance optimized next-generation AI infrastructure [2] [8].

official_insights.title: GPT-5.2-Codex Introduction
official_insights.brief_summary: OpenAI has introduced GPT-5.2-Codex, a advanced agentic coding model for professional software engineering and defensive cybersecurity [1]. This model is optimized for agentic coding in Codex and includes improvements on long-horizon work through context compaction, stronger performance on large code changes, and improved performance in Windows environments [1]. GPT-5.2-Codex is expected to enhance the capabilities of OpenAI's Codex platform [1]. The model is being rolled out gradually, with paid ChatGPT users having access to it first [1].

official_insights.title: AWS Partnership
official_insights.brief_summary: OpenAI has announced a multi-year strategic partnership with AWS to provide world-class infrastructure to run and scale OpenAI's core artificial intelligence (AI) workloads [2]. The partnership will enable OpenAI to access AWS compute comprising hundreds of thousands of state-of-the-art NVIDIA GPUs [2]. This collaboration is expected to enhance OpenAI's AI capabilities and provide cost and performance optimized next-generation AI infrastructure [2]. The partnership will also enable OpenAI to expand its capabilities in areas such as natural language processing and computer vision [2].

trusted_insights.title: Broadcom Partnership
trusted_insights.brief_summary: OpenAI has partnered with Broadcom to deploy 10 gigawatts of OpenAI-designed AI accelerators [8]. The collaboration will power breakthroughs in AI and bring the technology's full potential closer to reality [8]. The partnership will also enable OpenAI to embed what it has learned from developing frontier models and products directly into the hardware, unlocking new levels of capability and intelligence [8]. The partnership is expected to have a significant impact on the development of AI technology [8].

trusted_insights.title: Investment in Merge Labs
trusted_insights.brief_summary: OpenAI has invested in Merge Labs, which will accelerate research and development in bioengineering, neuroscience, and device engineering [7]. The investment will also enable Merge Labs to benefit from AI operating systems that can interpret intent, adapt to individuals, and operate reliably with limited and noisy signals [7]. The investment is expected to have a significant impact on the development of AI technology in these areas [7].

cross_source_analysis: The sources suggest that OpenAI is taking a comprehensive approach to the development of AI technology, including the introduction of new products, partnerships, and investments [1] [2] [8]. The company's focus on safety and security is evident in its approach to deploying GPT-5.2-Codex [1]. The sources also highlight the potential impact of the partnerships on the development of AI technology, including the potential for breakthroughs in areas such as natural language processing and computer vision [2] [8].

references.title: Introducing GPT-5.2-Codex - OpenAI
references.snippet:
We're releasing GPT‑5.2-Codex today in all Codex surfaces for paid ChatGPT users, and working towards safely enabling access to GPT‑5.2-Codex for API users in the coming weeks. In parallel, we’re piloting invite-only trusted access to upcoming capabilities and more permissive models for vetted professionals and organizations focused on defensive cybersecurity work. We believe that this approach to deployment will balance accessibility with safety.

## Pushing the frontier on real-world software engineering [...] Switch to

 ChatGPT(opens in a new window)
 Sora(opens in a new window)
 API Platform(opens in a new window)

OpenAI

December 18, 2025

ProductRelease

# Introducing GPT-5.2-Codex

The most advanced agentic coding model for professional software engineering and defensive cybersecurity.

$ npm i -g @openai/codex

Today we’re releasing GPT‑5.2-Codex, the most advanced agentic coding model yet for complex, real-world software engineering. GPT‑5.2-Codex is a version of GPT‑5.2⁠ further optimized for agentic coding in Codex, including improvements on long-horizon work through context compaction, stronger performance on large code changes like refactors and migrations, improved performance in Windows environments, and significantly stronger cybersecurity capabilities. [...] By rolling GPT‑5.2-Codex out gradually, pairing deployment with safeguards, and working closely with the security community, we’re aiming to maximize defensive impact while reducing the risk of misuse. What we learn from this release will directly inform how we expand access over time as the software and cyber frontiers continue to advance.

 2025
 OpenAI Codex

references.title: AWS and OpenAI announce multi-year strategic partnership - OpenAI
references.snippet: Today, Amazon Web Services (AWS) and OpenAI announced a multi-year, strategic partnership that provides AWS’s world-class infrastructure to run and scale OpenAI’s core artificial intelligence (AI) workloads starting immediately. Under this new $38 billion agreement, which will have continued growth over the next seven years, OpenAI is accessing AWS compute comprising hundreds of thousands of state-of-the-art NVIDIA GPUs, with the ability to expand to tens of millions of CPUs to rapidly scale agentic workloads. AWS has unusual experience running large-scale AI infrastructure securely, reliably, and at scale–with clusters topping 500K chips. AWS's leadership in cloud infrastructure combined with OpenAI's pioneering advancements in generative AI will help millions of users continue to get [...] The infrastructure deployment that AWS is building for OpenAI features a sophisticated architectural design optimized for maximum AI processing efficiency and performance. Clustering the NVIDIA GPUs—both GB200s and GB300s—via Amazon EC2 UltraServers on the same network enables low-latency performance across interconnected systems, allowing OpenAI to efficiently run workloads with optimal performance. The clusters are designed to support various workloads, from serving inference for ChatGPT to training next generation models, with the flexibility to adapt to OpenAI's evolving needs. [...] The rapid advancement of AI technology has created unprecedented demand for computing power. As frontier model providers seek to push their models to new heights of intelligence, they are increasingly turning to AWS due to the performance, scale, and security they can achieve. OpenAI will immediately start utilizing AWS compute as part of this partnership, with all capacity targeted to be deployed before the end of 2026, and the ability to expand further into 2027 and beyond.

   For Developers [...] Aardvark represents a breakthrough in AI and security research: an autonomous agent that can help developers and security teams discover and fix security vulnerabilities at scale. Aardvark is now available in private beta to validate and refine its capabilities in the field.

How Aardvark works

Aardvark continuously analyzes source code repositories to identify vulnerabilities, assess exploitability, prioritize severity, and propose targeted patches.

references.title: Built to benefit everyone - OpenAI
references.snippet:
Health and curing diseases. The OpenAI Foundation will fund work to accelerate health breakthroughs so everyone can benefit from faster diagnostics, better treatments, and cures. This will start with activities like the creation of open-sourced and responsibly built frontier health datasets, and funding for scientists.
   Technical solutions to AI resilience.Just as the internet required a comprehensive cybersecurity ecosystem—protecting power grids, hospitals, banks, governments, companies, and individuals—we now need a parallel resilience layer for AI. The OpenAI Foundation will devote resources to support practical technical solutions for AI resilience, which is about maximizing AI’s benefits and minimizing its risks. [...] Our Research

Support [...] This recapitalization was completed after nearly a year of engaging in constructive dialogue with the offices of the Attorneys General of California and Delaware. We made several changes as a result of those discussions and we believe OpenAI—and as a result, the public we serve—are better for them.

The path forward

The OpenAI Foundation and OpenAI Group will work in concert to advance solutions to hard problems and opportunities posed by AI progress. This includes making intelligence a tool that everyone can benefit from, building safe and aligned systems, turbocharging scientific discovery, and strengthening global cooperation and resilience.

references.title: OpenAI and Broadcom announce strategic collaboration to deploy 10 gigawatts of OpenAI-designed AI accelerators - OpenAI
references.snippet:
“Our partnership with OpenAI continues to set new industry benchmarks for the design and deployment of open, scalable and power-efficient AI clusters,” said Charlie Kawwas, Ph. D., President of the Semiconductor Solutions Group for Broadcom. “Custom accelerators combine remarkably well with standards-based Ethernet scale-up and scale-out networking solutions to provide cost and performance optimized next generation AI infrastructure. The racks include Broadcom’s end-to-end portfolio of Ethernet, PCIe and optical connectivity solutions, reaffirming our AI infrastructure portfolio leadership.”

For Broadcom, this collaboration reinforces the importance of custom accelerators and the choice of Ethernet as the technology for scale-up and scale-out networking in AI datacenters. [...] San Francisco and Palo Alto—October 13, 2025—OpenAI and Broadcom today announced a collaboration for 10 gigawatts of custom AI accelerators. OpenAI will design the accelerators and systems, which will be developed and deployed in partnership with Broadcom. By designing its own chips and systems, OpenAI can embed what it’s learned from developing frontier models and products directly into the hardware, unlocking new levels of capability and intelligence. The racks, scaled entirely with Ethernet and other connectivity solutions from Broadcom, will meet surging global demand for AI, with deployments across OpenAI’s facilities and partner data centers. [...] “Broadcom’s collaboration with OpenAI signifies a pivotal moment in the pursuit of artificial general intelligence,” said Hock Tan, President and CEO of Broadcom. “OpenAI has been in the forefront of the AI revolution since the ChatGPT moment, and we are thrilled to co-develop and deploy 10 gigawatts of next generation accelerators and network systems to pave the way for the future of AI.”

“Our collaboration with Broadcom will power breakthroughs in AI and bring the technology’s full potential closer to reality,” said OpenAI co-founder and President, Greg Brockman. “By building our own chip, we can embed what we’ve learned from creating frontier models and products directly into the hardware, unlocking new levels of capability and intelligence.”
